---
title: "Why Vulnerability MTTR Alone Misleads"
subtitle: "Add MOVA to Measure Real Risk"
author: "Caleb Kinney"
date: ""
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import polars as pl
from great_tables import GT, loc, style
from plotnine import (
    aes,
    coord_cartesian,
    element_blank,
    element_line,
    element_rect,
    element_text,
    geom_label,
    geom_line,
    geom_point,
    ggplot,
    labs,
    scale_color_manual,
    scale_x_continuous,
    theme,
    theme_minimal,
)

metrics = pl.read_parquet("data/metrics.parquet").with_columns(
    pl.col("strategy")
    .replace(
        {
            "oldest_first": "Oldest-First",
            "newest_first": "Newest-First",
        }
    )
    .alias("strategy_label")
)
summary = pl.read_parquet("data/summary.parquet").with_columns(
    pl.col("strategy")
    .replace(
        {
            "oldest_first": "Oldest-First",
            "newest_first": "Newest-First",
        }
    )
    .alias("strategy_label")
)

STRATEGY_COLORS = {
    "Oldest-First": "#1F6F78",
    "Newest-First": "#8B1E3F",
}
MONTH_BREAKS = [1, 6, 12, 18, 24]
LAST_MONTH = metrics.select(pl.col("month_index").max()).item()
ROOM_BG = "#f3ebde"
PANEL_BG = "#fffdf8"
GRID_COLOR = "#c8baa5"
MUTED_TEAL = "#8ea1a4"
MUTED_ROSE = "#b58a96"
chart_theme = (
    theme_minimal(base_size=22)
    + theme(
        figure_size=(11.35, 5.25),
        legend_position="none",
        panel_grid_minor=element_blank(),
        panel_grid_major_x=element_blank(),
        panel_grid_major_y=element_line(color=GRID_COLOR, size=0.7),
        panel_background=element_rect(fill=PANEL_BG, color=None),
        plot_background=element_rect(fill=ROOM_BG, color=None),
        axis_title=element_text(color="#26333A", size=18),
        axis_text=element_text(color="#26333A", size=14),
        axis_text_x=element_text(margin={"t": 6}),
        axis_text_y=element_text(margin={"r": 6}),
        plot_title=element_text(weight="bold", color="#101828", size=24),
        plot_subtitle=element_text(color="#55606B", size=15, margin={"b": 10}),
        plot_margin=0.22,
        axis_title_x=element_text(margin={"t": 14}),
        axis_title_y=element_text(margin={"r": 14}),
    )
)

card_lookup = {
    row["strategy_label"]: row
    for row in summary.select(
        "strategy_label",
        pl.col("mttr_days").round(1).alias("mttr_days"),
        pl.col("mova_days").round(1).alias("mova_days"),
        "open_count",
        "aged_over_180",
    ).to_dicts()
}

oldest_mttr = f'{card_lookup["Oldest-First"]["mttr_days"]:.1f}'
oldest_mova = f'{card_lookup["Oldest-First"]["mova_days"]:.1f}'
oldest_open = int(card_lookup["Oldest-First"]["open_count"])
oldest_tail = int(card_lookup["Oldest-First"]["aged_over_180"])

newest_mttr = f'{card_lookup["Newest-First"]["mttr_days"]:.1f}'
newest_mova = f'{card_lookup["Newest-First"]["mova_days"]:.1f}'
newest_open = int(card_lookup["Newest-First"]["open_count"])
newest_tail = int(card_lookup["Newest-First"]["aged_over_180"])
```

## MTTR just doubled {.center .hook-slide}

:::: {.shock-card}
::: {.shock-label}
Mean time to remediate
:::

:::: {.shock-row}
::: {.shock-panel}
::: {.shock-caption}
Last month
:::
::: {.shock-value}
15d
:::
:::

::: {.shock-arrow}
→
:::

::: {.shock-panel .danger}
::: {.shock-caption}
This month
:::
::: {.shock-value}
31d
:::
:::
::::

::: {.shock-subtitle}
Dashboards signal failure here. The better question is: what happened to exposure, the aging risk still sitting open?
:::
::::

---

## This actually happened

::: {.callout .callout-important}
MTTR increased after we launched a program.
:::

::: {.incremental}
- MTTR was baselined as a KPI
- The number went up
- Leadership asked: “What changed?”
- From the dashboard, it looked like we got worse
:::

::: {.kicker}
We were closing older backlog that had been sitting for a long time.
:::

---

## Why this looks like failure

::: {.incremental}
- MTTR increased
- Longer time to remediate closed work
- Interpreted as slower performance
- Interpreted as declining team effectiveness
:::

::: {.kicker}
The mistake is assuming one closure metric describes the whole backlog.
:::

---

## The paradox {.center .statement-slide}

::: {.metric-big .centered}
MTTR can rise

while backlog age falls
:::

::: {.paradox-cue}
::: {.paradox-pill}
<span class="paradox-name">MTTR</span>
<span class="paradox-trend">↑</span>
:::
::: {.paradox-connector}
while
:::
::: {.paradox-pill}
<span class="paradox-name">MOVA</span>
<span class="paradox-trend paradox-trend-down">↓</span>
:::
:::

::: {.callout}
Same team. Same capacity. Different prioritization.
:::

---

## Flow vs. stock

::: {.definition-grid}
::: {.definition-card}
::: {.eyebrow}
Flow
:::
### MTTR
How fast are we closing work?

Time to remediate **closed** vulnerabilities.
:::

::: {.definition-card}
::: {.eyebrow}
Stock
:::
### MOVA
How old is the work still open?

**Mean Open Vulnerability Age**

How old is the vulnerability backlog we still carry?
:::
:::

::: {.kicker}
It measures closure speed, not the age of the backlog that remains.

It measures the age of the backlog you still own today.
:::

---

## What do we mean by exposure?

::: {.incremental}
- Older vulnerabilities tend to carry more risk over time
- Long-lived backlog increases uncertainty and attack surface
- Reducing the age of the backlog reduces accumulated exposure
:::

::: {.kicker}
Age is not just time. It is unaddressed risk.

Once closed work and open work are separated, the exposure question becomes much easier to see.
:::

---

## Averages hide the tail

::: {.tail-visual}
::: {.tail-visual-caption}
Most findings stay young. A small tail gets very old.
:::
<div class="tail-strip" role="img" aria-label="A tight cluster of younger findings on the left, with a small number of very old findings separated on the right.">
  <div class="tail-cluster">
    <span class="tail-dot"></span><span class="tail-dot"></span><span class="tail-dot"></span><span class="tail-dot"></span><span class="tail-dot"></span><span class="tail-dot"></span><span class="tail-dot"></span><span class="tail-dot"></span><span class="tail-dot"></span><span class="tail-dot"></span><span class="tail-dot"></span><span class="tail-dot"></span><span class="tail-dot"></span><span class="tail-dot"></span><span class="tail-dot"></span><span class="tail-dot"></span><span class="tail-dot"></span><span class="tail-dot"></span><span class="tail-dot"></span>
  </div>
  <div class="tail-cluster tail-cluster-outlier">
    <span class="tail-dot tail-dot-outlier"></span>
  </div>
</div>
<div class="tail-axis">
  <span>Low age</span>
  <span>Very high age</span>
</div>
:::

::: {.tail-example-grid}
::: {.tail-card}
::: {.tail-number}
95
:::
::: {.tail-text}
findings at 7 days
:::
:::

::: {.tail-card}
::: {.tail-number}
5
:::
::: {.tail-text}
findings at 400 days
:::
:::

::: {.tail-card .emphasis}
::: {.tail-text .strong}
The average looks fine.
:::
::: {.tail-text}
The oldest findings are not moving.
:::
:::
:::

---

## Report the full picture

::: {.signal-grid}
::: {.signal-card}
::: {.signal-name}
MTTR
:::
::: {.signal-meaning}
Closure speed
:::
:::

::: {.signal-card}
::: {.signal-name}
MOVA
:::
::: {.signal-meaning}
Backlog age
:::
:::

::: {.signal-card}
::: {.signal-name}
Open Count
:::
::: {.signal-meaning}
Backlog size
:::
:::

::: {.signal-card}
::: {.signal-name}
180+ Days
:::
::: {.signal-meaning}
Aging tail
:::
:::
:::

---

## MTTR measures closed work {auto-animate="true"}

**MTTR**

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
#| code-line-numbers: "2|3-5|6"
mttr = (
    vulns.filter(pl.col("resolved_at").is_not_null())
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("resolved_at") - pl.col("created_at")).dt.total_days().alias("age_days")
    )
    .select(pl.col("age_days").mean().alias("mttr_days"))
)
```

::: {.kicker}
Closed work. Time to remediate. Age at closure.
:::

---

## MOVA measures open work {auto-animate="true"}

**MOVA**

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
#| code-line-numbers: "2|3-5|6"
mova = (
    vulns.filter(pl.col("resolved_at").is_null())
    .with_columns(
        (pl.datetime_now() - pl.col("created_at")).dt.total_days().alias("age_days")
    )
    .select(pl.col("age_days").mean().alias("mova_days"))
)
```

::: {.kicker}
Open work. Current age. Age still carried today.
:::

---

## You already have the data

::: {.incremental}
- MTTR comes from your tools
- MOVA uses the same data
- For open findings, age = today minus created date
- Take the average across open findings
- A CSV export is usually enough
:::

::: {.kicker}
Same data. Different population. Different age.
:::

---

## Why “did we fix it in time?” falls short

::: {.incremental}
- SLA views are useful
- But they tell you whether you met a deadline
- They say less about the exposure you still carry now
- MOVA shows the age of the backlog still open today
:::

---

## A simple simulation

::: {.incremental}
- Same arrivals
- Same fix rate
- Same time horizon
- Start with the same backlog
- Add the same incoming vulnerabilities each month
- Apply the same remediation capacity each month
- Change only the order of work: newest-first vs. oldest-first
:::

::: {.kicker}
To isolate that effect, keep capacity constant and change only prioritization.

Now watch what each metric rewards.
:::

---

## MTTR rewards recent closures

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-alt: "Line chart of MTTR over time under two prioritization strategies. Newest-First is highlighted and finishes with the lower MTTR because it closes recent findings first."
mttr_plot = metrics.select(
    "month_index",
    "strategy_label",
    pl.col("mttr_days").alias("days"),
)
mttr_labels = (
    mttr_plot.filter(pl.col("month_index") == LAST_MONTH)
    .with_columns(
        pl.format(
            "{} {}d",
            pl.col("strategy_label"),
            pl.col("days").round(0).cast(pl.Int64),
        ).alias("label")
    )
)

(
    ggplot(mttr_plot, aes(x="month_index", y="days", color="strategy_label"))
    + geom_line(size=2.35)
    + geom_point(data=mttr_labels, size=3.8)
    + geom_label(
        data=mttr_labels,
        mapping=aes(label="label"),
        ha="left",
        nudge_x=0.6,
        size=10,
        fill=PANEL_BG,
        label_padding=0.15,
        label_r=0.12,
    )
    + scale_color_manual(
        values={
            "Newest-First": STRATEGY_COLORS["Newest-First"],
            "Oldest-First": MUTED_TEAL,
        }
    )
    + scale_x_continuous(breaks=MONTH_BREAKS)
    + coord_cartesian(xlim=(1, 28.6))
    + labs(
        title="Newest-First wins on MTTR",
        subtitle="Closing recent findings first keeps closure age low.",
        x="Month",
        y="MTTR (days)",
    )
    + chart_theme
)
```

::: {.centered .muted}
On MTTR, Newest-First wins.
:::

---

## MOVA shows backlog age

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-alt: "Line chart of MOVA over time under two prioritization strategies. Oldest-First is highlighted and finishes with the lower open-backlog age because it removes older findings first."
mova_plot = metrics.select(
    "month_index",
    "strategy_label",
    pl.col("mova_days").alias("days"),
)
mova_labels = (
    mova_plot.filter(pl.col("month_index") == LAST_MONTH)
    .with_columns(
        pl.format(
            "{} {}d",
            pl.col("strategy_label"),
            pl.col("days").round(0).cast(pl.Int64),
        ).alias("label")
    )
)

(
    ggplot(mova_plot, aes(x="month_index", y="days", color="strategy_label"))
    + geom_line(size=2.35)
    + geom_point(data=mova_labels, size=3.8)
    + geom_label(
        data=mova_labels,
        mapping=aes(label="label"),
        ha="left",
        nudge_x=0.6,
        size=10,
        fill=PANEL_BG,
        label_padding=0.15,
        label_r=0.12,
    )
    + scale_color_manual(
        values={
            "Oldest-First": STRATEGY_COLORS["Oldest-First"],
            "Newest-First": MUTED_ROSE,
        }
    )
    + scale_x_continuous(breaks=MONTH_BREAKS)
    + coord_cartesian(xlim=(1, 28.6))
    + labs(
        title="Oldest-First lowers backlog age",
        subtitle="Working the oldest backlog first lowers the age still carried.",
        x="Month",
        y="MOVA (days)",
    )
    + chart_theme
)
```

::: {.centered .muted}
On MOVA, Oldest-First wins.
:::

---

## The 180+ tail tells the truth

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-alt: "Line chart of open vulnerabilities older than 180 days under two prioritization strategies. Oldest-First is highlighted and reduces the tail, while Newest-First leaves the old backlog stranded."
tail_plot = metrics.select(
    "month_index",
    "strategy_label",
    "aged_over_180",
)
tail_labels = (
    tail_plot.filter(pl.col("month_index") == LAST_MONTH)
    .with_columns(
        pl.format(
            "{} {}",
            pl.col("strategy_label"),
            pl.col("aged_over_180"),
        ).alias("label")
    )
)

(
    ggplot(tail_plot, aes(x="month_index", y="aged_over_180", color="strategy_label"))
    + geom_line(size=2.35)
    + geom_point(data=tail_labels, size=3.8)
    + geom_label(
        data=tail_labels,
        mapping=aes(label="label"),
        ha="left",
        nudge_x=0.6,
        size=10,
        fill=PANEL_BG,
        label_padding=0.15,
        label_r=0.12,
    )
    + scale_color_manual(
        values={
            "Oldest-First": STRATEGY_COLORS["Oldest-First"],
            "Newest-First": MUTED_ROSE,
        }
    )
    + scale_x_continuous(breaks=MONTH_BREAKS)
    + coord_cartesian(xlim=(1, 28.6))
    + labs(
        title="Oldest-First reduces the 180+ tail",
        subtitle="Newest-First keeps almost the entire old backlog alive.",
        x="Month",
        y="Open findings over 180 days",
    )
    + chart_theme
)
```

::: {.centered .muted}
This is the backlog that stays stranded.
:::

---

## End state: the tail tells the story

::: {.score-grid .tail-grid}
::: {.score-card .score-good}
::: {.score-title}
Oldest-First
:::
::: {.score-metric}
`{python} oldest_tail`
:::
::: {.score-label}
findings older than 180 days
:::
:::

::: {.score-card .score-bad}
::: {.score-title}
Newest-First
:::
::: {.score-metric}
`{python} newest_tail`
:::
::: {.score-label}
findings older than 180 days
:::
:::
:::

::: {.centered .muted}
Under Newest-First, the old backlog barely moves.
:::

---

## Same data, wrong winner

```{python}
#| echo: false
decision_table = (
    summary.with_columns(
        pl.when(pl.col("strategy") == "oldest_first")
        .then(pl.lit("Recommended: best backlog outcome"))
        .otherwise(pl.lit("MTTR-only winner"))
        .alias("decision"),
        pl.when(pl.col("strategy") == "oldest_first")
        .then(pl.lit(0))
        .otherwise(pl.lit(1))
        .alias("sort_order"),
    )
    .sort("sort_order")
    .select(
        "strategy_label",
        pl.col("mttr_days").round(1).alias("mttr_days"),
        pl.col("mova_days").round(1).alias("mova_days"),
        "open_count",
        "aged_over_180",
        "decision",
    )
)

(
    GT(decision_table, rowname_col="strategy_label")
    .tab_header(
        title="Same data, wrong winner",
        subtitle="Newest-First wins on MTTR. Oldest-First wins on backlog age.",
    )
    .tab_spanner(label="Flow", columns="mttr_days")
    .tab_spanner(label="Backlog Age", columns=["mova_days", "aged_over_180"])
    .tab_spanner(label="Backlog", columns="open_count")
    .tab_spanner(label="Interpretation", columns="decision")
    .cols_label(
        mttr_days="MTTR",
        mova_days="MOVA",
        open_count="Open count",
        aged_over_180="180+ tail",
        decision="Interpretation",
    )
    .cols_align(align="left", columns=["decision"])
    .cols_align(
        align="center",
        columns=["mttr_days", "mova_days", "open_count", "aged_over_180"],
    )
    .fmt_number(columns=["mttr_days", "mova_days"], decimals=1)
    .fmt_integer(columns=["open_count", "aged_over_180"])
    .tab_style(
        style=[style.fill("#f7e7ec"), style.text(color="#6b1230", weight="bold")],
        locations=loc.body(
            columns="mttr_days",
            rows=pl.col("strategy_label") == "Newest-First",
        ),
    )
    .tab_style(
        style=[style.fill("#fbf0f4"), style.text(color="#7a2941", weight="bold")],
        locations=loc.body(
            columns="decision",
            rows=pl.col("strategy_label") == "Newest-First",
        ),
    )
    .tab_style(
        style=[style.fill("#e4f0ee"), style.text(color="#134b52", weight="bold")],
        locations=loc.body(
            columns=["mova_days", "aged_over_180", "decision"],
            rows=pl.col("strategy_label") == "Oldest-First",
        ),
    )
    .tab_style(
        style=style.text(weight="bold"),
        locations=loc.stub(),
    )
    .tab_style(
        style=style.text(weight="bold"),
        locations=loc.column_labels(),
    )
    .opt_align_table_header(align="left")
    .opt_row_striping()
    .opt_vertical_padding(scale=1.1)
    .opt_horizontal_padding(scale=1.0)
)
```

::: {.kicker}
Read only MTTR and you pick the wrong winner.
:::

---

## This is not anti-MTTR

::: {.incremental}
- MTTR is still useful
- It shows how efficiently work is completed
- It helps identify process friction
:::

::: {.kicker}
The problem is not MTTR.

It is using MTTR alone.
:::

---

## The paradox repeats across teams

::: {.incremental}
- The same pattern appears across teams
- Team A has a cleaner backlog and faster closures
- Team B has an older backlog and slower closures
- If the oldest backlog sits in Team B, effort should shift there
:::

---

## Clean up the oldest backlog

::: {.incremental}
- Remediation effort shifts to Team B
- MTTR rises because older work is finally closing
- MOVA falls because the oldest backlog is finally shrinking
- Exposure drops even though the headline metric looks worse
:::

::: {.kicker}
MTTR shows flow. MOVA reveals aging exposure.
:::

---

## When leaders see only MTTR {auto-animate="true"}

::: {.dashboard-grid}
::: {.dashboard-card .winner}
::: {.dashboard-title}
Newest-First
:::
::: {.dashboard-value}
`{python} newest_mttr` days
:::
::: {.dashboard-label}
MTTR
:::
:::

::: {.dashboard-card}
::: {.dashboard-title}
Oldest-First
:::
::: {.dashboard-value}
`{python} oldest_mttr` days
:::
::: {.dashboard-label}
MTTR
:::
:::
:::

::: {.centered .muted}
If this is the only KPI, leaders reward the strategy that closes recent work fastest.
:::

---

## When leaders see the full system {auto-animate="true"}

::: {.dashboard-grid}
::: {.dashboard-card .dashboard-detail}
::: {.dashboard-title}
Newest-First
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>MTTR</span>
<strong>`{python} newest_mttr` days</strong>
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>MOVA</span>
<strong>`{python} newest_mova` days</strong>
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>Open count</span>
<strong>`{python} newest_open`</strong>
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>180+ days</span>
<strong>`{python} newest_tail`</strong>
:::
:::

::: {.dashboard-card .dashboard-detail .winner}
::: {.dashboard-title}
Oldest-First
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>MTTR</span>
<strong>`{python} oldest_mttr` days</strong>
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>MOVA</span>
<strong>`{python} oldest_mova` days</strong>
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>Open count</span>
<strong>`{python} oldest_open`</strong>
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>180+ days</span>
<strong>`{python} oldest_tail`</strong>
:::
:::
:::

::: {.centered .muted}
MTTR still matters. It cannot stand alone.
:::

---

## Read the signals together

::: {.incremental}
- MTTR up + MOVA down = old backlog is getting cleared
- MTTR down + MOVA up = recent closures look better while backlog age worsens
- Across teams, MTTR up can mean effort shifted to older backlog
- 180+ up = aging backlog is getting stranded
:::

---

## What to report

::: {.incremental}
- Put MTTR and MOVA on the same monthly view
- Add open count and a threshold such as 180+ days
- Label the MTTR time window before calling a regression
- Use the full set of metrics to explain cleanup work when old findings close
- Define both metrics in code before they reach the dashboard
- Show one chart and one table that make exposure visible
:::

---

## Change the review

::: {.incremental}
- Report MTTR and MOVA side by side
- Track `180+ days open` as a separate backlog line
- Change the review so exposure is visible before a judgment call is made
:::

---

## Real data, not just dashboards

You do not need a new platform for this.

Most teams already have the data. It is usually sitting in a CSV.

::: {.incremental}
- A simple export is enough
- CSVs are messy and unavoidable
- Dashboards often hide the metric definition
- Analysts need to own the definition in code
:::

::: {.kicker}
This is not a tooling problem first. It is a definition problem first.
:::

---

## Add data science to your toolbox

- Define metrics in code, not dashboards
  _Python with Polars_

- Show how risk changes over time
  _Plotnine for clear charts_

- Make backlog state obvious at a glance
  _Great Tables for summaries_

- Keep analysis and narrative together
  _Quarto for reviewable reports_

- Explore the data behind the metric
  _Positron or your preferred IDE_

::: {.kicker}
This is data storytelling for security.
If you do not define the metric, the dashboard will.
:::

---

## Can AI do this for me?

::: {.incremental}
- AI can speed up transforms, charts, and first drafts
- The metric definition still needs an owner
- Use AI to move faster
- Keep the work in code so it stays auditable and repeatable
:::

::: {.kicker}
The implementation details matter less than changing what gets reviewed.
:::

---

## A simple policy

::: {.metric-mid}
Track MTTR and MOVA together.

Prioritize reducing the oldest backlog.
:::

::: {.kicker}
Do not reward teams for keeping MTTR low while backlog ages.
:::

---

## Add MOVA. Keep MTTR in context. {.center .statement-slide}

::: {.metric-big .centered}
Lead with MOVA for exposure.

Use MTTR for flow.

Read them together, not alone.
:::

---

## MTTR alone drives the wrong call {.center .statement-slide}

::: {.metric-big .centered}
If you optimize for MTTR alone,

you keep investing in teams
that already look healthy,

while aging backlog remains elsewhere.

That is the wrong decision.
:::

---

## Read the system, not just the metric {.center .statement-slide}

::: {.metric-big .centered}
MTTR shows flow.

MOVA shows exposure.

<br>

github.com/cak/mttr-vs-mova
:::
